This is a heavy testimony. Ali says that of this entire world, they held nothing but this land. It wasn't about the soil or the date palms; it was about the **Sanctity of the Prophetic Gift**. How was Fadak given to Fatima? When the verse was revealed:
And give the near of kin their due [Quran 17:26]
the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) called Fatima. Gabriel had told him:
Give Fatima Fadak.

This was not an inheritance that came after death; it was a **gift during his lifetime** (Nahlah). Yet the "Reality of the World" wanted to portray it as a disputed inheritance to apply their fabricated rule: **"Prophets do not leave inheritance."** By seizing Fadak, they were not just taking land; they were attempting to cut the financial lifeblood of the Imamate and humiliate the daughter of the Messenger.

They asked her for "witnesses" for a gift given by the Prophet! Does the "Leader of the Women of Paradise" need a witness for her truthfulness? The Quran says she is purified, yet they demanded witnesses to prove she wasn't lying. This is the "Chain of Lies" returning again. They rejected the testimony of Ali, and they rejected the testimony of her children, and they rejected her own word. This was the intellectual and political siege that preceded the physical assault.
Segment 3: The Logic of the Sermon and the Role of Aisha

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on the famous "Fadak Sermon" (al-Khutbah al-Fadakiyyah) delivered by Lady Fatima (SA) in the Prophet's Mosque. He describes the scene of her arrival, noting that her walk was identical to that of her father, which caused a state of emotional upheaval among the people. The Sheikh discusses how she used pure Quranic logic to dismantle the Caliph's fabrications, specifically highlighting her citations of the inheritance of previous Prophets. He also touches upon the "Document of Aisha," explaining how the testimony of the Prophet's wife was utilized to contradict Lady Fatima’s claims, further cementing the divide between the "Reality of the Progeny" and the "Political Reality" of the state.

The third point in this area is a glance at the **Sermon of Fadak**. Look at the atmosphere of this sermon. When Fatima (peace be upon her) decided to go to the mosque, she did not go as a mere "claimant" for property. She went as a **Manifestation of the Prophet**. The narrators say:
She put on her veil and her cloak, and she walked... and by Allah, her walk did not differ from the walk of the Messenger of Allah.
This wasn't just physical; it was a spiritual signal to the people that the one addressing them was the **Soul of Muhammad**.

When she entered the mosque and a curtain was drawn for her, she let out a single **Sigh**. That sigh was so heavy with grief and truth that the entire mosque erupted in wailing. People who had been silent or complicit could not hold back their tears. She silenced them and began her speech—a speech that is the **Alphabet of the Doctrine**. She didn't start with land. She started with **Tawhid**, **Prophecy**, and the **Wisdom of the Law**. Then she turned to them and said:
Are you now claiming that I have no inheritance? Is it in the Book of Allah that you inherit from your father, but I do not inherit from my father? Indeed, you have brought a monstrous thing!
She challenged them with the Quran. She cited the inheritance of **Solomon from David**, and the prayer of **Zechariah** for a son who would inherit from him. She asked them:
Did Allah reveal a verse specifically excluding my father? Or do you say that people of two different religions do not inherit from each other? Am I and my father not of the same religion?

This is the **Logic of the Infallible**. She exposed that their "Hadith" (Prophets do not leave inheritance) was a fabrication designed to override the Quran. It was a "theological weapon" used to strip her of her status.

And here we must mention the role of **Aisha**. The documents show that Aisha was one of the primary voices used to support the Caliph's claim. They used the "Mothers of the Believers" against the "Leader of the Women of Paradise." While Fatima was citing the Quran, they were citing a "private memory" that only the Caliph and Aisha seemed to share. This was the "Game of Sources" in its earliest, most lethal form. The "Reality of the World" needed Aisha's testimony to give a religious cover to a political theft. This created a rift that remains in the Ummah today. On one side, you have the **Quran and Fatima**; on the other, you have the **Fabricated Hadith and the Caliphate**.

Fadak was not an orchard. In the language of the Imams, Fadak is the **Wilayah** (Imamate). When the later Caliphs offered to return Fadak to the Imams, the Imams would define its boundaries as the entire Islamic world! Why? Because if you admit she was right about the land, you admit she was right about the **Leadership**. The "Slanders and Lies" were the only way they could keep their thrones.
Segment 4: The Symbolism of Fadak and Political Advisors

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the aftermath of the Fadak sermon, highlighting how the Caliph was momentarily moved by Lady Fatima's logic but was ultimately steered back by the political advisors around him. He details the theological claim made by Aisha regarding inheritance and how it was used to neutralize the Quranic evidence Lady Fatima had presented. The Sheikh explains that in the eyes of the Ahl al-Bayt, Fadak was never about material wealth; it was a symbol for the entire Islamic world and the right to lead it. He describes how this "theological theft" was a necessary precursor to the physical marginalization of Imam Ali (AS).

The sermon was not a plea for help; it was a trial. Fatima (peace be upon her) placed the entire Ummah in the witness stand. When she finished, the silence in the mosque was the silence of defeat. The documents tell us that Abu Bakr began to weep and said:
O daughter of the Messenger, you are the truth, and your father was the truth.
But the **Reality of the World** has a guard. Umar stood up and whispered to the Caliph:
If you give her Fadak today, your authority is over. You will have admitted that she is right and you are wrong, and that Ali is the true successor.

This is the political calculation. The truth was seen as a threat to the "Security of the State." Then came the use of **Aisha**. We find the reports explaining that Aisha was one of the primary reasons Lady Fatima was denied her inheritance. She provided the testimony that "supported" the Caliph's claim. They turned the house of the Prophet against itself. They used the status of one wife to strike the status of the only daughter.
Fatima’s logic was based on the **Universal Quran**, while their logic was based on a **Private Hadith** that no one else had heard. She said to them:
Does the Quran say Solomon inherited from David, or are you saying Solomon was not a Prophet?

They had no answer. But in the world of power, an answer is not needed when you have the sword. The Sheikh clarifies the true definition of the land. **Fadak isn’t merely an orchard; it is the Imamate.** In a famous narration, when Harun al-Rashid asked Imam al-Kadhim to define the boundaries of Fadak so he could return it, the Imam said:
The first boundary is Aden, the second is Samarkand, the third is Africa, and the fourth is the seas and the islands.
The Sultan was shocked and said:
Then you are asking for my entire kingdom!
The Imam replied:
I told you, if I define its true boundaries, you will never return it.

This is the "Reality of Religion." Fadak was the symbol of the **Total Authority** of the Ahl al-Bayt. By taking the orchard, they were declaring that the family of the Prophet had no right to anything in this world—neither land, nor leadership, nor even safety.
Segment 5: The Assassination Plot in the Mosque

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi reveals one of the most dangerous papers in the File: the premeditated plot to assassinate Imam Ali (AS) inside the mosque. He explains that after the Fadak sermon, the Saqifa party realized that as long as Ali remained alive, their authority was fragile. The Sheikh describes the secret meeting between the leaders and Khalid ibn al-Walid, who was assigned the task of killing the Imam during the morning prayer. He details the specific plan where Khalid was to stand next to Ali and strike him once the Caliph gave a specific signal at the end of the prayer. This document serves as proof that the "Reality of the World" was prepared to commit the ultimate sacrilege—murdering the Successor of the Prophet in the House of Allah—to protect their political project.

We now move to a paper that exposes the "Terrorist Side" of the Saqifa. This is not just about a land dispute; this is the **Plot to Assassinate the Commander of the Faithful**. The documents record that after the sermon of al-Zahra, the atmosphere in Medina became volatile. People were whispering, and the legitimacy of the new order was shaking. Abu Bakr and Umar met in private. Umar said:
As long as this man [Ali] is alive, you will not be secure on this pulpit. He must be removed.

They called for **Khalid ibn al-Walid**. Khalid was the "Sword of the State," the man who was willing to carry out the orders that others found too heavy. They made an agreement: Khalid would attend the morning prayer and stand right next to Ali. The plan was specific: Abu Bakr would lead the prayer. Khalid would have his sword hidden under his cloak. When Abu Bakr reached the final **Taslim** (the end of the prayer), he would give a signal, and at that moment, Khalid would draw his sword and strike Ali while he was still sitting.

Think about the "Reality of the World" here. They were planning a murder in the **Prophet's Mosque**, during the **Morning Prayer**, while they were standing before Allah! This shows you that they had no fear of Allah; they only feared losing the "Bride" (the World). As the prayer began, the documents say that Abu Bakr started to feel the weight of what he was doing. He was a man who often wavered when faced with the direct consequences of his actions. As he was reciting the prayer, he began to think about the aftermath. He realized that the Shi'a of Ali and the Hashimites would not remain silent; Medina would turn into a bloodbath.

The entire prayer passed in a state of terror. Khalid was waiting, his hand on the hilt of his sword, watching Abu Bakr’s mouth, waiting for the signal. Abu Bakr sat for the final **Tashahhud**, and the moment for the **Taslim** arrived. But Abu Bakr could not say it. He sat in silence for a long time. The people behind him were wondering why the Imam of the prayer was not finishing. Finally, out of fear, before saying the Salam, he spoke words that were not part of the prayer:
O Khalid! Do not do what I commanded you!
Then he said:
Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah.
Segment 6: The Reaction of Imam Ali and Theological Slander

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi describes the immediate aftermath of the failed assassination attempt. He details Imam Ali's (AS) reaction, specifically his physical confrontation with Khalid ibn al-Walid, and the subsequent "theological warfare" launched by the Saqifa party. The Sheikh presents a painful document from the teachings of Imam al-Sadiq (AS), which reveals that the enemies did not stop at physical assault but moved to strike the honor of Lady Fatima (SA). He explains how accusations were made from the pulpits against her character to justify their seizure of Fadak and their rejection of her testimony, framing this as a "Second Martyrdom" of the Prophet's daughter.

When Abu Bakr shouted those words—"O Khalid, do not do what I commanded you!"—the mosque was filled with confusion. People looked at one another, wondering what "command" was being cancelled in the middle of the prayer. The documents record that Imam Ali (peace be upon him) did not wait. He turned to Khalid, who still had his hand on his sword, and seized him. He threw him to the ground with such force that the people thought Khalid's bones would break. Ali looked at the two leaders and said:
Is this the 'Sunnah' of your Prophet? To kill the one he called his brother in his own mosque?

They had no answer but silence. But don't think they stopped there. When the "Sword" failed, they turned to the **"Tongue."** This is the most bitter paper in the File. Sheikh al-Ghizzi refers to a narration from our Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). The Imam says that they were not content with taking the land or breaking the rib. They wanted to destroy her **Status** in the eyes of the Ummah.
**Lady Fatima was accused of fornication (al-Fahisha) from their pulpits.**

Listen to the pain of the Infallible! Why did they do this? Because when she demanded Fadak, and they asked for witnesses, they began to insinuate that she was a "liar." And from calling the daughter of the Prophet a "liar," they moved to the most heinous of slanders. They used their "official media"—the Friday pulpits—to suggest that her claims were those of a woman who had lost her moral compass.

They did this to justify why they had rejected the **Verse of Purification**. They wanted to tell the people: "See? She is just an ordinary woman, prone to sin and error like anyone else." This was the "Intellectual Murder" of al-Zahra. It was a calculated move to ensure that the "Reality of the World" would never be threatened by her "Reality of Religion" ever again. This is why we say the File of Fatima is not just about a door. It is about a **System of Oppression** that used every weapon: the sword, the fire, the stolen land, and the poisoned tongue. They tried to bury her name under a mountain of slanders, but the "Light of Allah" cannot be extinguished by the breath of liars.
Segment 7: The Enforcer: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba

Sheikh al-Ghizzi transitions to a detailed examination of Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, whom he identifies as one of the primary "enforcers" and "criminals" of the Saqifa party. He describes Mughira as a man with a dark moral history, including a well-documented incident of adultery that was covered up by the authorities. The Sheikh explains that this lack of character made Mughira the perfect tool for the "Reality of the World" to use against the "Reality of Religion." He focuses on a specific historical document where Mughira, years later in the court of Mu'awiyah, attempted to verbally abuse Imam Hassan (AS), only to be met with a devastating public indictment. Imam Hassan reminded the court that Mughira was the one who physically struck Lady Fatima (SA) so severely that it led to the miscarriage of her child and her ultimate martyrdom.

Let us look at the "Tools of Power." Who were the men who stood around the pulpit? They weren't the men of piety; they were the men of the sword and the men of the "Dark Past." At the top of this list is **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba**. Mughira was a man whose history was so dark that even the early chroniclers couldn't hide it. He was a man accused of **Zina** (adultery) with four witnesses testifying against him. But because he was a "Vessel of Secrets" for the leadership, the legal punishment was avoided through a technicality provided by the Caliph.

Why is this important for the **File of Fatima**? Because a man who has no regard for the laws of Allah in his private life will have no regard for the "Leader of the Women of Paradise" in his public life. He was the "hitman" of the project. He was the one who was willing to strike when others hesitated. In a famous document from **Mu'awiyah's court**, Mughira tried to gain favor with the new Umayyad Sultan by insulting Imam Hassan (peace be upon him). He stood up and began to speak ill of Imam Ali and Lady Fatima, trying to reduce them to "rebels" against the state.
Imam Hassan (AS) rose with the dignity of his father. He didn't just defend himself; he exposed the **Crime Document** of the past. He looked at Mughira in front of the entire assembly and said:
And you, O Mughira! You are the one who struck Fatima, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, until she bled and miscarried what was in her womb!
The Imam said:
You did this to humiliate the Messenger of Allah and to oppose his command regarding her love.

This is a public accusation by an Infallible Imam. It confirms that the assault wasn't just a "push at the door"; it was a targeted, physical attack by men like Mughira who used their whips and their strength to break the core of the Prophetic family. Mughira’s silence after this indictment is his confession. The File of Fatima records his name in the ink of blood. He is the symbol of the **Lowness of the Attackers** versus the **Sublimity of the Attacked**. If you follow the "Sunnah" of Mughira, you are an enemy of the "Path of Fatima."
Segment 8: Institutionalized Abuse and Zaid ibn Ali

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the "culture of abuse" that was institutionalized against the Ahl al-Bayt, specifically focusing on the public cursing and belittling of Lady Fatima (SA) through the centuries. He links this historical animosity to the tragedy of Zaid ibn Ali, explaining how the Umayyad authorities used the same insults against Lady Fatima to justify the brutal execution of her descendants. He argues that the "File of Fatima" is a recurring tragedy where the descendants are punished for the "Sin of Loyalty" to the Prophet's daughter, proving that the original slanders were intended to have a permanent, lethal effect on the status of the Progeny.

We must understand that the assault on the house was not an isolated event. It was the birth of a **Culture of Abuse**. We note that the "Reality of the World" established a protocol where Lady Fatima’s name was mentioned with derision or intentional neglect from the pulpits of the Umayyads for decades. They did not just take the land; they took the **Honor of the Name**. Let us look at the incident of **Zaid ibn Ali ibn al-Hussain**. When Zaid rose against the tyranny of the Umayyads, he was not just fighting for a political cause; he was defending the honor of his grandmother, al-Zahra.

The documents of history record that when Zaid was brought before the tyrant **Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik**, the Sultan did not insult Zaid directly. Instead, he chose the most painful weapon. We see that he began to **insult Lady Fatima** in front of Zaid. He spoke words that no Muslim should ever utter about the "Leader of the Women of Paradise," questioning her status and her truthfulness. Zaid (peace be upon him) wept and said:
By Allah, if Muhammad were alive, would you dare speak of his daughter this way in front of his grandson?

But the "Reality of the World" has no shame. This incident proves that the **File of Fatima** was the tool used by the Caliphs to break the spirit of the Ahl al-Bayt’s followers. If they could desecrate the mother, they could destroy the sons.

We see the theological connection. If they could make the people believe that Fatima was "ordinary" or "mistaken" in the first century, then they could justify killing her grandsons in the second and third centuries. The "Slanders and Lies" of the Saqifa became the "Arrows and Swords" of the Umayyads and Abbasids. This is why the Ziyarah says: **"The one who killed you killed Islam."** Every drop of blood shed from the neck of Zaid ibn Ali, and every drop shed in Karbala, can be traced back to the blood that was spilled at the door of Fatima. The criminals are the same, and the motive is the same: the hatred for the "Prophetic Light" that refuses to submit to the "Darkness of the World."
Segment 9: The Veracity of the Progeny vs. The Umayyad Slander

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens the exploration of the "verbal assault" on Lady Fatima (SA) by examining the historical court of Mu'awiyah. He details the specific dialogue where Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba attempted to humiliate Imam Hassan (AS) through insults aimed at his parentage, which Imam Hassan countered by exposing Mughira's role in the physical attack on al-Zahra. The segment then moves to the Umayyad era, documenting how public cursing of Lady Fatima became a systematic tool to demoralize her descendants. The Sheikh recounts the tragic encounter between Zaid ibn Ali and the Caliph Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik, where the Caliph deliberately used heinous slanders against Lady Fatima to provoke and break the spirit of her grandson, illustrating that the "File of Fatima" remained a weapon of psychological warfare long after her death.

The documents of history show us that the "Reality of the World" never stopped at the door of the house. The assault continued through the tongues of the "Sultans' men." Let us return to the court of Mu'awiyah, where **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba** was sitting. Mughira thought he could use the pulpit to strike the **Status of the Progeny**. He began to speak about Imam Ali and Lady Fatima, using language that was meant to suggest they were "seekers of the world" or "rebels against the unity." But Imam Hassan (peace be upon him) was present.
The Infallible does not allow the Truth to be trampled. Imam Hassan rose and looked at Mughira—the man whose own life was a series of scandals—and said:
You speak of my mother? You, whom the Prophet cursed? You are the one who struck her side and broke her rib! Your hands are stained with the blood of her miscarriage!

This public indictment stripped Mughira of his mask. It shows that the "File of Fatima" was the primary defense of the Ahl al-Bayt against the liars. If you claim to be a Muslim, how can you sit with a man who admits to striking the daughter of your Prophet?

But the slanders did not end with Mughira. We move to a darker page: **The Swearing at Lady Fatima**. For nearly eighty years, the pulpits of the Umayyads were used to curse Imam Ali. But many people do not know that Lady Fatima was also a target of this institutionalized abuse. They wanted to erase the **Memory of her Purity**. If the people remembered that she was "The Leader of the Women of Paradise," they would realize that those sitting on the throne were usurpers. So they had to create a "New Fatima"—a woman who was "angry and stubborn" or "mistaken in her claims."

Look at the document of **Zaid ibn Ali**. When Zaid, the son of Imam al-Sajjad, was brought before **Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik**, the Caliph knew he couldn't defeat Zaid's logic. So he used the ultimate weapon of the Saqifa: the **Slander of al-Zahra**. The Caliph began to use foul language against Lady Fatima, saying:
What has the daughter of Muhammad brought us but rebellion?
and used even more graphic insults that I cannot repeat out of respect for her sanctity. Zaid wept and said:
By Allah, you have violated the Prophet in his grave!
The Caliph’s response was:
Her status died with her father; today, only the sword of the state remains.

This is the "Reality of the World." They believed that once the Prophet was gone, his daughter's honor was "fair game" for their politics. The File of Fatima records these insults as the **Second Martyrdom** of the Prophet's daughter.
Segment 10: The Abbasid Malice: Abu Ja'far al-Mansur

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on the Abbasid era, specifically the reign of Abu Ja'far al-Mansur (al-Dawaniqi). He highlights how the Abbasids, who initially rose to power using the slogan "The Pleasing one from the Family of Muhammad," became even more brutal towards the descendants of Lady Fatima (SA) than the Umayyads had been. The Sheikh provides documents showing how al-Mansur used the pulpit to publicly belittle Lady Fatima's status as a "mere woman" to deny the political and spiritual claims of her descendants. He argues that this systematic devaluation was a "re-opening" of the original wounds of the Saqifa, proving that both the Umayyad and Abbasid "Realities of the World" were unified in their mission to suppress the "Reality of the Progeny."

We move from the "Omelette of the Umayyads" to the "Fire of the Abbasids." If the Umayyads were the enemies who struck from the front, the Abbasids were the ones who betrayed from within. They used the name of the Ahl al-Bayt to steal the "Bride," and once they sat on the throne, they turned against the **Children of Fatima**. Look at the document regarding the Abbasid Caliph **Abu Ja'far al-Mansur**. He was a man of extreme malice toward the Fatimid lineage. When the Hasanid Sayyids rose in revolt, al-Mansur did not just fight them with soldiers; he fought them with the **"Weapon of Slander"**—the same weapon forged at Saqifa.
There is a recorded speech where al-Mansur stood on the pulpit and said:
What is the status of Fatima? She was but a woman. Does a woman inherit the Imamate? Does a woman grant legitimacy?

He was trying to decouple the **Prophetic Blood** from the **Prophetic Authority**. He wanted to convince the Ummah that being a descendant of Fatima carried no spiritual weight. By calling her "but a woman," he was echoing the words of the first Caliph who said she was "just a claimant" and the words of Umar who said "Even so!" regarding her presence in the house.

The documents show that al-Mansur went so far as to write letters to **Imam al-Sadiq** and the leaders of the revolts, mocking the fact that they took pride in a "female ancestor." This is the **Masculine Arrogance of the World** trying to extinguish the **Divine Light of the Womb of al-Zahra**. The "File of Fatima" records that under al-Mansur, the descendants of the Prophet were walled up alive in pillars of salt or buried in the foundations of buildings. Why such cruelty? Because their very existence was a reminder of the **Original Crime**. As long as a "Fatimid" existed, the Abbasid throne was a house built on sand.

They realized that to kill the sons, they had to "kill" the mother again—not with a door this time, but with **Theology**. They created a school of thought that marginalized the "Woman of Paradise" into a domestic figure with no relevance. This is the "Abbasid Poison" that still exists in many books today. The File of Fatima is here to pull her name out from those salt pillars and return her to the center of the Universe.
Segment 11: Persecution, Al-Mutawakkil, and Final Reflection

In the final segment of Episode 5, Sheikh al-Ghizzi connects historical slanders to the physical persecution of the Fatimid descendants. He details the uprising of Muhammad ibn al-Imam al-Sadiq and the treatment of the Prophet's family by the Abbasid Caliph al-Mutawakkil, who attempted to erase the memory of the Ahl al-Bayt through the destruction of Imam Hussain's grave. The Sheikh concludes with a deeply emotional personal reflection and a "monologue with al-Zahra," asserting that while everything in his life might be subject to error, his Love for Fatima remains the only absolute truth that will save him.

The "Reality of the World" reached its peak of madness in the era of the Abbasid Caliph **al-Mutawakkil**. If al-Mansur used theology to strike at Fatima, al-Mutawakkil used the **pickaxe and the plow**. The documents show that every revolution led by the descendants of Fatima—like that of **Muhammad the son of Imam al-Sadiq**—was fueled by the same fire: the refusal to let the slanders against their mother become the "Official Islam."

Al-Mutawakkil's crimes were not just against the living, but against the dead. We find the record of his order to **raze the grave of Imam Hussain**. He ordered the land to be plowed and water to be diverted over it so that no trace of the "Son of Fatima" would remain. Why did he do this? Because as long as the "Grave of the Oppressed" existed, the "Throne of the Oppressor" was in danger. He wanted to finalize the crime that started at the door of the house.

But look at the end of the tyrants and the end of the "Leader of Women." The Abbasids are in the trash-heap of history, while millions of hearts beat for the name of Fatima. I address you, O Mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussain: This is a page from the File that has been folded by the "Culture of Negligence," but we are here to unfold it. I say to you, O daughter of Muhammad: Everything I do is the "craft of the helpless." I have no power to give you your right. I am a man of many sins and errors.

I may lie about every single thing in my life I may lie in every claim I make except in one thing: **My Love for You, O Mother of the Pure Ones**. This love is my brain, my logic, and my very life. It is the breath that allows me to exist. Without your love, my heart is silent and my soul is void. This is my only hope: that when the "Time of the Departing Soul" comes, and I am placed in that dark grave, I will see you coming toward me with glad tidings. This is the hope of every "Fatimid." We live by your pain, and we hope to die by your pleasure. I am a friend to those who love you and an enemy to those who oppose you.
